jobs.title: English Teacher - AI Trainer
jobs.jd: DataAnnotation is committed to creating quality AI. Join our team to help train AI chatbots while gaining the flexibility of remote work and choosing your own schedule.We are looking for a bilingual professional to join our team and teach AI chatbots. You will have conversations in both Hindi and English with chatbots in order to measure their progress, as well as write novel conversations in order to teach them what to say.Benefits:This is a full-time or part-time REMOTE positionYou’ll be able to choose which projects you want to work onYou can work on your own scheduleProjects are paid hourly, starting at $22+ USD per hour, with bonuses for high-quality and high-volume workResponsibilities (both in Hindi and English):Come up with diverse conversations over a range of topicsWrite high-quality answers when given specific promptsCompare the performance of different AI modelsResearch and fact-check AI responsesQualifications:Fluent in Hindi + EnglishA bachelor's degree (completed or in progress)Excellent writing and grammar skillsStrong research and fact-checking skills to ensure accuracy and originalityNote: Payment is made via PayPal. We will never ask for any money from you. PayPal will handle any currency conversions from USD.#INDHIARJob Type: FreelancePay: From ₹1,971.58 per hourWork Location: Remote

jobs.title: Teachers/Subject Matter Expert
jobs.jd:
The teacher will be responsible for presenting and explaining learning materials and educating students on new subjects. The teacher will communicate their subject matter by using lectures, printed, visual, audio and/or video materials. They must create a curriculum, form daily lesson plans, and prepare all lesson materials. In orders to educate their class and ensure success, teachers should maintain a positive attitude, be energetic about the subjects they teach, and share a passion for learning themselves.
Qualifications:
Good subject knowledge and an understanding of Current education requirements
A compassionate and confident leader.
Very strong verbal and written communication skills.
Good at problem-solving quickly under pressure.
Excellent collaborator.
Outstanding critical thinking abilities.
Strong organizational skills.
Developing educational content such as video lectures, notes, PPTs and quizzes etc.
To plan and structure individual learning experience, including curriculum mapping, scheme of work and lesson plans.
To prepare sessions which is build on previous learning and which encourages students to question.
To identify appropriate teaching and learning techniques using appropriate assessment methods to measure learning and achievements.
To select and develop resources to ensure well – prepared interesting and relevant learning.
Extensive knowledge of Microsoft Office suite.
Experience with web programs like Blackboard or other online course management, assignment-posting systems.
A passion for learning and sharing your knowledge with others.

Email-Id:hr@samadhan.group
Job Type:Full-time

jobs.jd: Academic Leadership:· Plan, implement, and review the subject curriculum in alignment with school and board guidelines.· Ensure effective lesson planning and delivery across all classes.· Maintain consistency in teaching standards and methodologies.Teacher Management:· Supervise, mentor, and support subject teachers.· Conduct regular classroom observations and provide constructive feedback.· Assist in teacher training, development, and performance evaluation.Curriculum & Assessment:· Design and review question papers, worksheets, and assessment tools.· Ensure timely completion of syllabus and proper academic planning.· Analyze student performance and implement improvement strategies.Student Development:· Monitor academic progress of students and address learning gaps.· Support remedial programs and enrichment activities.· Encourage participation in subject-related competitions and activities.Coordination & Communication:· Coordinate with school leadership, coordinators, and other departments.· Communicate effectively with parents regarding student performance when required.· Organize subject meetings and maintain documentation.Administrative Responsibilities:· Maintain academic records, reports, and documentation.· Ensure compliance with school policies and academic standards.· Assist in timetable planning and resource allocation.Qualifications & Skills:· Master’s degree in the relevant subject (B.Ed. preferred).· Minimum 3–5 years of teaching experience; leadership experience preferred.· Strong subject knowledge and classroom management skills.· Excellent communication, leadership, and organizational abilities.· Familiarity with modern teaching tools and technology.Key Competencies:· Leadership & Team Management· Planning & Organization· Analytical Thinking· Communication Skills· Problem SolvingJob Type: Full-timePay: ₹35,000.00 - ₹40,000.00 per monthBenefits:Health insuranceWork Location: In person

programs, webinars, and certification sessions as required.Document training feedback and submit timely reports to stakeholders.Key Requirements:Bachelor’s degree in Engineering (preferably in Electronics, Computer Science, Mechatronics, or Robotics) or equivalent technical background.2+ years of experience in training/teaching or working with educational robotics and AI platforms.Strong understanding of robotics kits, microcontrollers (Arduino, Raspberry Pi, etc.), sensors, actuators, and AI tools.Ability to simplify complex technical concepts for a non-technical audience.Willingness to travel locally or regionally to conduct training/workshops.Preferred Skills (Good to Have):Experience working with AI tools like Python, machine learning libraries, TensorFlow, etc.Experience with STEM/STEAM education environments.Familiarity with educational tools like Scratch, Blockly, or other block-based coding platforms.Certification in AI, Robotics, or STEM Training.What We Academic LeadershipSupervise curriculum planning and implementation in line with CBSE guidelines.Monitor lesson delivery, assessments, and exam preparation.Ensure academic rigor and consistency across subjects.Introduce innovative teaching strategies and digital learning tools.2. Faculty ManagementLead and mentor subject teachers and department heads.Conduct teacher evaluations, classroom observations, and professional development programs.Facilitate collaboration among faculty for curriculum enrichment.3. Student AchievementTrack and analyze student performance data.Implement remedial programs and enrichment activities.Guide students in career counseling, competitive exam preparation, and skill development.4. Administrative DutiesCoordinate academic schedules, timetables, and examination processes.Ensure compliance with CBSE policies, school regulations, and accreditation requirements.Prepare academic reports for the Principal and management.5. Parent & Community EngagementCommunicate academic progress and initiatives to parents.Organize orientation programs, workshops, and academic events.Build good relationships with external organizations for academic enrichment.6. jobs.title: Classroom Teacher
jobs.jd: JOB DESCRIPTION:We are seeking a self-motivated, personable, and flexible College Graduate Coordinator (CGC) based in Bangalore.Shanti Bhavan, a world-class English-medium residential school accredited by ICSE and ISC, is seeking:Trained Montessori teacher for Pre-Kindergarten.Trained Chemistry teacher with a postgraduate degree for secondary grades. Trained Economics teacher with a postgraduate degree for secondary grades.Trained English teacher with a postgraduate degree for middle grades. ORGANIZATION & JOB BRIEF:Shanti Bhavan is a highly reputed, internationally known institution that transforms children from socially and economically deprived backgrounds into professionals and leaders of society.Appointed teachers may be placed at either of our two residential school campuses, both located approximately 2 hours from Bangalore city. The first campus is in Baliganapalli, Tamil Nadu, bordering Karnataka, and the second campus is in Dyavarahalli, North Bangalore.We have been featured in major publications, including the New York Times, BBC, Outlook, and Glamour. We are in the NETFLIX documentary Daughters of Destiny.COMPENSATION & WORK ENVIRONMENT:Excellent salary, bonus, and Provident Fund.Free housing, meals, and housekeeping services on campus. Teachers are required to live on campus.Family quarters on campus for senior staff with extended family.Free education for the children of staff at Shanti Bhavan.Small classes (maximum 28 students per class)International standards.State-of-the-art resources, including computers and Internet.Quiet, secure, pollution-free environment; 30 acres of lush green, secure campus.Opportunity to strive for social reform.REQUIREMENTS:Bachelor’s degreeFull professional fluency in English + strong written and verbal communication skillsStrong interpersonal skills (personable; trustworthy; empathetic; good listener)Highly organized, motivated, self-starterTech adaptable; working knowledge of Google Workspace (including Gmail, Google Drive, Google Calendar, and Google Meet)We seek energetic individuals with a passion for teaching and the ability to motivate their students. Recent college graduates with TTC, B. Ed, or M. Ed may also apply for lower grades, while those with Master’s degrees and experienced teachers are invited to apply for higher grades.Applications are being accepted for June 2026 appointments.Married applicants wishing to live together on campus must both be qualified to apply.Interested?Please complete an interest form here:https://form.asana.com/?k=Qd4qmoamd2No1UBnYGeniA&d=1174282405894102For further information about the school, you may visit www.shantibhavanchildren.orgJob Type: Full-timePay: From ₹25,000.00 per monthBenefits:Flexible schedulePaid time offProvident FundAbility to commute/relocate:Bengaluru, Karnataka: Reliably commute or planning to relocate before starting work (Required)Application Question(s):Are you Montessori trained (for Preschool Teacher applicants)Do you possess a post-graduate degree (for Chemistry, English, Economics)?Education:Bachelor's (Required)Experience:Teaching: 3 years (Preferred)Work Location: Hybrid remote in Bengaluru, Karnataka

(Students currently pursuing their graduation are not eligible.)● Experience: Prior teaching or mentoring experience is a plus.● Skills: ○ Strong communication and interpersonal skills.○ Ability to connect with students and explain concepts clearly.○ Creative teaching style with a passion for transforming education.○ Strong problem-solving abilities.● Comfortable working in the US time zone (03:30 AM – 08:30 AM IST).What We’re Looking For At Codeyoung, we’re not just seeking teachers who transfer knowledge — we want mentors who inspire, engage, and transform learning. If you believe in making education exciting, impactful, and creative, this role is for you.Job Type: Part-timeExpected hours: 30 per weekWork Location: In person

Job Description
Archdiocesan Catholic schools in San Francisco,Marin, and Mateo County are seeking exceptional teacher(s). A valid teaching credential, along with a minimum of two years of teaching experience, is preferable. To meet the needs of our growing communities, we are looking for a talented, creative, hard-working, team player with a broad range of skills, along with an appreciation of the developmental characteristics of youth, to join our strong faculty. Providing excellence in Catholic education since 1954, deep-rooted traditions and current best practices, the school(s) are dedicated to educating the whole child so that he or she may be successful in the 21st century. Educators focus on teaching students to be thoughtful, spiritual, and nurturing individuals, highly effective communicators, and involved knowledge seekers.

The school community is seeking qualified PreSchool, Middle School, and High School Teacher(s) for the 2026-2027 school year. We offer full/part-time positions. Our Mission Guided by Catholic values, our Catholic Schools are committed to educating each individual based on the teachings of Jesus Christ. We advocate academic excellence and inspire spiritual, intellectual, and social growth. We prepare our diverse community of students to become citizens committed to service and stewardship.

The ideal candidate must have the ability to develop engaging and innovative lessons that allow our students to grow academically, socially, and emotionally. Incorporating technology and hands-on learning experiences into the classroom with enthusiasm, as well as coordinating and working with both administration and colleagues, is an expectation. Strong communication skills, both orally and in writing as well as excellent classroom management skills are required. The ideal candidate is committed to excellence and supports and upholds.

Application materials may be downloaded from the official DCS website by clicking on the following link: www.sfarchdiocese.org/employment
About the Employer
The Department of Catholic Schools (DCS) provides support to over 24,200 students and 2,287 teachers, principals, and school staff who are on their faith and education journey. Our purpose is to promote and serve our Community of Catholic Schools and the Archdiocese of San Francisco by communicating, assessing and reporting on matters of faith, Catholic Identity, policy and academic achievement between the Archdiocese, DCS, and the schools.

Catholic schools in the Archdiocese of San Francisco, in collaboration with parents, pastors, parish leadership, and the faith community, teach and model Gospel values and the faith of the Catholic Church thereby forming witnesses of Jesus Christ through comprehensive religious, academic, co-curricular and service programs.

jobs.title: Principal Search
jobs.jd:
All Souls Catholic School
San Franscico, CA
Job ID#:
16004174176
Posted:
March 12, 2026
Expires:
May 11, 2026
Category:
Primary Education
Terms:
Full-Time Employee
Experience:
0-2 Years
Education:
Graduate Degree
Base Pay:
$120k to $150k Salary
Send applications or inquiries to: escobarc@sfarch.org
Job Description
PRINCIPAL SEARCH PROSPECTUS
Archdiocese of San Francisco Reports to: Pastor and Superintendent of Schools
Grounded in the Catholic faith and committed to academic excellence, the Catholic schools of the Archdiocese of San Francisco cultivate the virtuous life, nurture Christian community, and nourish a Catholic worldview. The call to be a school leader is a call to not only professional, but spiritual leadership. It is a call to be a visionary and to lead teachers, building a "school of the Lord's service" that fully integrates the Catholic faith and academic excellence. It is also a call to live as a witness to the Gospel of Christ. A principal in a Catholic School is called to be a religious leader, instructional leader, administrator, communicator, and witness to Christian discipleship. These are important ministries within the Church, and not everyone has been given the gifts to respond to such a call.
SCHOOL MISSION STATEMENT: All Souls Catholic School is a diverse community working together to teach and live the Gospel message. We prepare our students, in a safe and supportive environment, to become active Christians, confident communicators, and fully integrated individuals. Our students develop the skills needed to move forward as life long learners and responsible global citizens.
PARISH MISSION STATEMENT:
All Souls Church is a Catholic Community of believers, Diverse in our individual gifts and cultural backgrounds, United in our one baptism in one Lord, and sharing in one faith. As the early church was commissioned by Jesus to proclaim the Word of God, we, too, are called to participate in that mission by endeavoring to be his witnesses. We witness to the Risen Christ by: celebrating our faith in worship, valuing and empowering our members, supporting one another through faith sharing, embracing gospel values by the teachings of the Catholic Church, and serving one another and the wider community.
ARCHDIOCESE OF SAN FRANCISCO EDUCATION MISSION STATEMENT:
Grounded in the Catholic faith and committed to academic excellence, the Catholic schools of the Archdiocese of San Francisco cultivate the virtuous life, nurture Christian community, and nourish a Catholic worldview.
ABOUT ALL SOULS CATHOLIC SCHOOL
Founded in 1949 and situated in fast-growing South San Francisco, All Souls Catholic School is a vibrant parish school serving approximately 200 students. Guided by its mission to teach and live the Gospel message, the school works in close partnership with All Souls Church to form students who are active in their Catholic Faith. The school is known for its strong sense of community, dedicated teachers, and commitment to academic excellence within a safe and supportive environment. This mission is further enriched by a robust weekend Faith Formation program that serves many parish families and reflects the parish's broader commitment to worship, service, and faith sharing across cultures and generations. Together, the school and parish seek to nurture students who grow in faith, character, and responsibility as global citizens.
As All Souls looks to its next principal, the community is seeking a leader who will honor the school's rich legacy and multi-generational traditions and strengthen its close-knit culture. The community hopes for a principal who will engage families and staff, with a commitment to building on the school's strong foundation of faith, community, and academic excellence, through transparency and clear communication. Key priorities include supporting teachers and helping the school grow through increased enrollment and long-term stability.
WHY LEAD A SCHOOL:
The Principal exists to lead a Catholic school as a community of faith, learning, and formation, ordered toward the truth, goodness, and beauty revealed in Jesus Christ.
This role advances the mission of the Archdiocese of San Francisco by:
Ensuring the school is unmistakably Catholic in culture and witness
Forming students intellectually, morally, and spiritually
Leading adults-faculty, staff, and families-into a shared vision of Catholic education
Sustaining the long-term vitality of the school through strong leadership, stewardship, and effective management
The call to be a Catholic school leader is not merely professional, but vocational. It requires spiritual maturity, intellectual seriousness, and the capacity to lead others with courage, clarity, and charity.
KEY OUTCOMES OF THE PRINCIPAL ROLE:
1. A Coherent and Lived Catholic Identity
The school operates as a visible and credible ministry of the Church, where faith in Jesus Christ is the animating principle of school life.
2. Strong Instructional and Academic Culture
Teaching and learning are marked by sound pedagogy, intellectual curiosity, joyful inquiry, academic seriousness, and a Catholic worldview that integrates faith and reason.
3. Healthy Faculty & Staff Culture and Leadership Capacity
Faculty and staff are well-formed, mission-aligned, and supported through clear expectations, professional growth, and spiritual leadership.
4. Trusting Partnership with Parents and Parish
Parents are respected as primary educators and engaged in ongoing formation through the school; the school and parish operate in genuine collaboration, with the pastor as the leader of the parish and school community.
5. Operational and Financial Stability
The school is well-managed, compliant, orderly, well-maintained, and positioned for long-term sustainability.
HOW SUCCESS IS MEASURED:
Catholic Identity
Consistent and frequent sacraments and communal prayer
Clear articulation of the school's Catholic mission to parents and faculty
Observable alignment between stated mission and daily practice
Academic & Instructional Leadership
High-quality classroom instruction, sound pedagogy rooted in Christian anthropology, and effective supervision
Coherent curriculum informed by the Catholic and Western intellectual tradition
Evidence of student growth and thoughtful use of various forms of assessments
Faculty & Staff Culture
Retention of mission-aligned faculty
Regular supervision, evaluation, and formative feedback
Positive staff morale and shared ownership of the mission
Intellectual and spiritual friendship among the faculty
Community & Governance
Productive working relationship with pastor, advisory bodies, and the Department of Catholic schools
Strong family engagement and confidence in leadership
Effective communication and conflict management
Operational Vitality
Balanced budgets and responsible financial stewardship
Compliance with Archdiocesan policies and legal requirements
Orderly, safe, and well-run school environment
IDEAL CHARACTER TRAITS
Faithful, practicing Catholic with integrity of life
A spirit of adventure and entrepreneurship
Spiritual maturity and humility
Courage to lead and make difficult decisions
Intellectual curiosity and love of learning
Sense of vocation and missionary discipleship
IDEAL COMPETENCIES
Spiritual and instructional leadership
Clear and compelling communication
Faculty supervision, coaching, and evaluation
Strategic and systems-level thinking
Relational leadership and conflict navigation
Organizational and operational management
Ability to lead change while preserving tradition

IDEAL CONTENT KNOWLEDGE
Catholic doctrine and Christian morality
The mission and history of Catholic education
Sound pedagogy and child development
Curriculum and assessment practices
School finance, budgeting, and governance
Relevant canonical, legal, and Archdiocesan policies

QUALIFICATIONS
Practicing Roman Catholic in full communion with the Church
Minimum three years of teaching and/or administrative experience
Master's degree (completed or in progress), preferably in education or liberal arts
Basic Catechist Certification (or commitment to complete)

SALARY & BENEFITS
Pay Range: $121,761-$234,431 annual base pay range. Credit for credentials, advanced degrees, and certifications are in addition to base salary.
Competitive salary aligned with Archdiocesan guidelines
Comprehensive healthcare coverage
Archdiocesan pension plan and optional 403(b)
Paid time off
Ongoing professional and spiritual formation
APPLICATION PROCESS
Application materials:
Completed application
Resume
Official transcripts
Proof of Catechist Certification (if obtained)
Three letters of reference
Written statement of philosophy of Catholic education (250-500 words) Physical requirements for the position of school principal include: Frequent computer use of keyboard and mouse (if applicable). Sometimes sitting at a computer for long periods of time. Frequent bending, reaching, stretching.

Qualified candidates will be invited to an initial interview with the Superintendent and HR Director prior to consideration by the School Search Committee.
Completed application packets should be send to Christine Escobar escobarc@sfarch.org

NOTICE OF NON-DISCRIMINATION Pursuant to the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, all employees of the Archdiocese of San Francisco shall be employed without regard to race, color, sex, ethnic or national origin and will consider for employment, qualified applicants with criminal histories
Send applications or inquiries to: escobarc@sfarch.org
About the Employer
The Department of Catholic Schools (DCS) provides support to over 24,200 students and 2,287 teachers, principals, and school staff who are on their faith and education journey. Our purpose is to promote and serve our Community of Catholic Schools and the Archdiocese of San Francisco by communicating, assessing and reporting on matters of faith, Catholic Identity, policy and academic achievement between the Archdiocese, DCS, and the schools.

Catholic schools in the Archdiocese of San Francisco, in collaboration with parents, pastors, parish leadership, and the faith community, teach and model Gospel values and the faith of the Catholic Church thereby forming witnesses of Jesus Christ through comprehensive religious, academic, co-curricular and service programs.

jobs.title: Secondary Teacher – English and Social Studies
jobs.jd:
We are looking for a highly-motivated, committed Teachers for Secondary section who are passionate about teaching and imparting quality education to children from marginalized communities.
Position: Teacher – English and Social Studies
Location: Bandra, Mumbai
Reports to: Head / Incharge
Brief about Aseema:
Aseema is a Mumbai-based NGO that is committed to equipping children from marginalised communities with high quality, value-based education, enabling the development of their limitless potential. We run three municipal schools in Mumbai in partnership with the Municipal Corporation of Greater Mumbai (MCGM), and one school for tribal children in Igatpuri. Aseema reaches out to 4,400 children living in vulnerable circumstances in urban slums and tribal communities.
Responsibilities and Duties:
To prepare innovative lesson plans and teaching aids on time.
To monitor the progress of children by correcting books and assignments regularly.
To maintain close links with co-curricular teachers and to coordinate with Social Worker and Counsellor for the children’s requirements.
To actively participate in the Parents meeting and provide feedback on the progress of the child to the parents.
To complete administrative responsibilities assigned on time.
To give equal attention to children of different abilities in the class.
To be attentive and observant of the children’s needs.
To motivate and enthuse the children.
To communicate with children in a respectful and positive manner.
To be flexible, open to change and willing to take on additional responsibilities.
Required Experience, Skills and Qualifications
Graduate / Post Graduate with B.Ed. in English and Social Studies.
Minimum 3 years of teaching experience with the Secondary section.
Excellent communication skills – both written and oral
Proficient in MS Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint).
Well versed with recent technologies and applications Teaching.
Interested candidates residing in Mumbai, please apply with current and expected salary and write to us on hr@aseema.org.Salary is not a constraint for the right candidate with desired qualification and experience.

Creative and able to create a fun learning environment.

jobs.title: IIT JEE NEET CET Teachers
jobs.jd:
WHY WORK WITH US?

Rathi’s Aspire Academy is one of the progressive coaching class offering coaching for IIT-JEE/NEET/CET, Foundation courses for Competitive exams and 8th-12th std. regular syllabus for CBSE, ICSE and State boards. It is a fast-growing institute due to blend of technology, quality teachers, and excellent management. It has earned name for itself in a very short span of time. The academy has nurtured an ecosystem where regular workshops, latest teaching tools and techniques, and advanced trainings are provided to teaching faculties. We welcome both full time and consultancy based teachers as we believe in quality teaching. Salary is no bar for a quality teachers. Apart from fixed/lecture based salary, we also provide rewards to teachers based on result they produce, students’ feedback, their promptness and their contribution in growth of the academy.

Do you want to work with us?

Rathi’s Aspire Academy is always on lookout for passionate and committed educators who can play an integral role in nurturing young minds.
Requirement
At least 3+ years of relevant teaching exp.
At least B.Tech /Post-graduate from IIT, NIT or similar grade colleges
Produced rankers/toppers in earlier assignments
 Responsibilities-
Taking online/offline classes for students
Deliver advanced topics in simple way
Identify smart/weak students and mentor them accordingly
Developing questions for the purpose of Assignments, Practice Questions, Test Papers and Daily Practice Worksheets etc.
Complete the entire syllabus of the designated batches well in time with full satisfaction of students, parents and the management
Keep upgrading the knowledge base by studying new and revised books on the subject

jobs.title: Assistant Teacher - Secondary Section (English and History)
jobs.jd:
Required Secondary Teachers with Post Graduation + B.ED course for teaching English and History should have minimum 2 years experience. Kindly upload your updated resume and B.ED certificate while applying for the Job.
C.N.M. SCHOOL & N. D. PAREKH PRE-PRIMARY SCHOOL was the first English medium school started by SVKM in 1997. It was the dream project of Late Shri Mukeshbhai Patel, since SVKM was running several colleges but had only one vernacular medium school.


His Mission was taken forward by Shri Balwantrai Sheth and Shri Vivek Vaidya. Mrs. Lata Munjal was the first Principal of the institution. Under the valuable guidance and support of Shri Amrishbhai Patel CNM school has achieved great progress in the field of academics and sports.The state of the art infrastructure has been planned and developed under the able guidance of Shri Bhupeshbhai Patel.

The school started with 25 students, 1 teacher and 1 support staff in a small place at Gokhalibai School,Vile Parle on June 1997. The school had only a Junior KG section with Mrs. Cheryl Mukath as their teacher.

The very next year the strength of the school grew to 154 students and by 2005 the strength was 3975 with eight divisions in each standard from nursery to standard Ten. After two years of its inception in 1999 the school got its new building, with the latest amenities for the students. The first batch of ICSE students passed out in 2003 with 22 students.


Mrs. Girija Mohan took over as the Principal from 2006 under whose stewardship, the school grew from strength to strength. In 2015, Mrs. Sangita Kukreti took over and the academics of the school got further cemented with latest technologies and new developments. In 2019, Mrs. Kavita Sanghvi stepped into the role of the Principal and introduced new concepts like ‘STEAM’, ‘Coding’ & ‘Global Outlook’ within the curriculum . Today there are 3131 students, 175 teaching staff, 12 office staff and 20 support staff who run the school with remarkable efficiency and dedication.
The school offers a modern, holistic and integrated system of education that moulds its students into free thinkers who can easily function as global citizens.

jobs.title: Social Studies Teacher
jobs.jd: A Social Studies Teacher educates students in history, geography, civics, economics, and sociology, fostering critical thinking, civic responsibility, and understanding of global/societal structures. They develop lesson plans, deliver engaging instruction, assess student progress, maintain classroom discipline, and collaborate with staff and parents.Key ResponsibilitiesInstruction & Curriculum: Create and implement comprehensive lesson plans that meet state and district standards in history, government, and social studies.Assessment & Evaluation: Prepare, administer, and grade assignments, quizzes, and tests to evaluate student progress.Classroom Management: Maintain an organized, respectful, and positive learning environment.Engagement: Facilitate class discussions, debates, and activities that promote critical thinking on historical and current events.Student Support: Differentiate instruction to accommodate diverse learning styles and support students' academic growth.Collaboration & Communication: Meet with colleagues, parents, and staff to discuss student progress and participate in school events.QualificationsEducation: Bachelor’s degree in Education, History, Social Studies, or a related field.Certification: Valid state teaching certification or licensure in Social Studies.Skills: Strong knowledge of social sciences, excellent communication, classroom management, and proficiency in educational technology tools.Typical Working ConditionsThis role usually involves working in middle or high schools.Requires standing for long periods and managing high-stress situations.Often includes extracurricular duties such as advising clubs (e.g., Debate Club) or coaching, notes.Job Types: Full-time, PermanentPay: ₹80,000.00 - ₹95,000.00 per monthBenefits:Commuter assistanceFlexible scheduleApplication Question(s):This job position is in Qatar ( Gulf Country). Are you willing to relocate to qatar?Do you have B.Ed?Work Location: In person

jobs.title: IGCSE Subject Teacher - Grade 2 (Math & English)
jobs.jd:
Job Information
Date Opened
04/10/2026
Job Type
Part time
Industry
Education
Work Experience
Fresher
Remote Job
Job Description
This is a remote position.
We are looking for a creative and dedicated Primary Educator to deliver the Cambridge Primary (Stage 3) curriculum for Grade 2. The ideal candidate will have a passion for foundational learning, helping students develop essential literacy and numeracy skills through inquiry-based and active learning methods. It is part time and work from home role.

Requirements
Key Responsibilities

English (Cambridge Primary English 0058)
Phonics & Literacy: Lead advanced phonics sessions and support students in transitioning to fluent reading.
Writing Skills: Teach students to plan writing through discussion and use basic punctuation (commas in lists, exclamation marks) and grammar (proper nouns, past/present tense).
Comprehension: Guide students in identifying main ideas, predicting story outcomes, and discussing character motivations.
Speaking & Listening: Foster a classroom environment where students speak fluently and confidently in varied contexts.
Mathematics (Cambridge Primary Mathematics 0096)
Number Sense: Teach counting, ordering, and place value for numbers up to 100.
Operations: Build mastery in addition and subtraction (up to two digits) and introduce multiplication (2x, 5x, 10x tables) as repeated addition.
Geometry & Measure: Teach identification of 2D and 3D shapes, lines of symmetry, and reading time to 5-minute intervals.
Fractions: Introduce the concept of halves and quarters as equal parts of a whole or a set.
Classroom Management & Assessment
Lesson Planning: Design engaging, age-appropriate lesson plans aligned with the Cambridge Progression Grid.
Student Tracking: Conduct regular assessments to monitor progress and identify learning gaps.
Parent Communication: Provide detailed feedback to parents regarding student academic and social development.
Activity-Based Learning: Use manipulatives, storyboards, and digital tools to make abstract concepts concrete.
Required Qualifications & Skills
Education: Bachelor’s degree in Education (B.Ed.) or a relevant subject.
Experience: Minimum 2 years of experience teaching Cambridge Primary/IGCSE curriculum.
Curriculum Knowledge: Strong familiarity with Cambridge Primary Stage 3 frameworks.
Soft Skills: High emotional intelligence, patience, and excellent verbal communication in English.

jobs.title: One - To - One Teacher - All Grades and Subjects
jobs.jd:
About us
We are a technology company that is obsessed with challenging conventional systems in education by leveraging the power of new-age technologies.
Our vision at Vedantu is to reimagine and evolve the way teaching and learning have been happening for decades. By combining quality teachers, engaging content, and superior technology, we are able to create a superior learning experience for students and aid in their outcome improvement, which is unlike any offline experience.
Our values
1. V > I #MilkeMachaateHain
2. V Operate With a #FounderMindset
3. #VInspire to Unlock Energies
4. V Are Humble and Have Empathy #BeHumble #HaveEmpathy
5. V Trust and Empower to Unleash Potential #VEmpower #VUnleashPotential
Work culture and perks
Communicate.
All broadband and mobile bills are reimbursed. We're after all a by-product of the mobile revolution!
Flexible office timings.
The team collectively decides what works for them. No dress code, an atmosphere of independence in decision-making.
Why join Vedantu?
Vedantu is India's leading Online tutoring company which enables personalized learning. Vedantu delivers quality education with cutting edge technology which brings India's Top Teachers and students together in a LIVE interactive e-classroom.
At Vedantu, Doubt Experts solve the doubt of students on vedantu doubt experts app by providing video solutions to students and also solve doubts on live call 1-1.
The company’s sole mission is to create a world of quality education that is easily accessible to all by providing better experience than group classes and customizable in terms of choices.
About the role!
Vedantu is looking for Teachers for their one to one teaching (one to one tuitions) wing as Freelance OTO Teacher Partners. This would require you to provide at least 4-5 hrs of free slots during the first half or second half of the day.You can choose from any one or more of the below mentioned preferable slots.
Preferable Slots
5AM to 9AM
9AM to 12PM
12 PM to 4PM
4PM to 8PM
8PM to 12AM
What will you be doing?

Take LIVE one to one coaching/Tutoring sessions with the students that are lined up for you while also ensuring that you maintain the required session ratings. You would be great at your job if you are able to ensure maximum conversions from Demo sessions or are able to provide maximum renewals of your existing students as this would mean that the students love what you teach and want to continue being mentored by you.
Ensure student stickiness for Vedantu which shall also be incentivised in terms of getting you a higher Per/hour rate. (You will be incentivised for being true Teacher partners in business)
Follow the session constructs & maintain the session ratings and the session quality at the desired thresholds
Following & adhering to any changes that may come up in the session constructs or policies from time to time based on business requirements
Who are we looking for?
Basic Requisite:
Through knowledge on Course curriculum
Doubt solving ability
Passion to Teach
Understanding Student expectations from the sessions
 You’re good at:
Advocating and Empathizing
Good communicator
Logic and problem solving
Team Player and Motivator
You’re extra amazing if:
High on energy
Passion towards students
Looking for a long term growth
Join the Vedan Family, Let’s make History - Be part of the Education
